project_reference.core_research_question: Can this buoyancy + hydrofoil engine produce at least 1.5W of shaft power for every 1W of air pumping input?
project_reference.current_focus: v1.1 milestone archived — planning next research stage (design changes required for v1.2)

scope.question: Is this buoyancy + hydrofoil engine net-positive at 1.5W output per 1W pumping input under reasonable component assumptions?
in_scope: Air pumping energy per vessel cycle
Buoyancy work during ascent at 3 m/s
Jet propulsion recovery from expanding air
Fill time feasibility (1/4 loop at 3 m/s)
unresolved_questions: Final loop geometry needed to compute fill window duration
Hydrofoil chord length and profile to assume for L/D estimate

user_asserted_anchors: Rough calculation suggests ~150% efficiency (1.5W out per 1W in)
Vessel rise velocity estimated at 3 m/s from buoyancy
Fill window = 1/4 loop circumference at 3 m/s
known_good_baselines: Pumping energy for single vessel: ~20.6 kJ isothermal, ~24.0 kJ adiabatic (from initial scoping)
context_gaps: Benchmark reference not yet selected; no published comparison for this specific buoyancy+hydrofoil engine type
crucial_inputs: User rough 150% efficiency estimate as target to validate or refute

formulations: Ideal gas law for air compression and expansion
allowed_fit_families: Standard hydrofoil L/D data for slow-speed water flow
forbidden_fit_families: Optimistic curve fits that assume perfect efficiency at any stage
stop_and_rethink_conditions: If complete system balance shows no plausible path to net positive output even under favorable assumptions

observables.name: System energy balance ratio (output/input)
observables.definition: Ratio of total shaft power output to air pumping power input across all 30 vessels

observables.name: Required air fill time per vessel
observables.definition: Time available to fill vessel with compressed air at 60 ft depth during 1/4 loop transit at 3 m/s

observables.name: Hydrofoil torque contribution per vessel
observables.definition: Net horizontal torque produced by ascending and descending vessels via tacked hydrofoil

observables.name: Net shaft power output
observables.definition: Total shaft power output minus pumping power input in watts

claims.statement: The system produces at least 1.5W of shaft output for every 1W of air pumping input under reasonable component assumptions
The vessel can be filled with the required air volume within the 1/4 loop window at 3 m/s

deliverables.description: Component-by-component energy balance table: pumping, buoyancy, hydrofoil torque, co-rotation drag reduction, jet recovery, losses
Summary table of all energy inputs and outputs per cycle with go/no-go verdict on 1.5W/W target
Fill time and flow rate analysis: required flow rate, available window, feasibility assessment

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-net-positive
acceptance_tests.procedure: Sum all energy inputs and outputs per cycle; compute output/input ratio; compare against 1.5W/W target
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Energy ratio >= 1.5 under reasonable (not optimistic) component assumptions

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-net-positive
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify all major loss mechanisms are accounted for: pump inefficiency, hydrofoil drag, bearing friction, co-rotation overhead
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No major loss category omitted from the energy balance

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-fill-feasible
acceptance_tests.procedure: Calculate fill window duration from loop geometry; calculate required air flow rate; check against practical equipment
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Required flow rate is achievable with practical equipment

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-net-positive
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Net positive buoyancy work alone without hydrofoil torque contribution
forbidden_proxies.reason: Buoyancy alone cannot exceed pumping energy; the gain comes from the hydrofoil L/D leverage

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-net-positive
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Individual components looking favorable without a complete system balance including all losses
forbidden_proxies.reason: Partial analysis can miss losses that eliminate the net positive result

weakest_anchors: Hydrofoil L/D ratio in low-speed water flow â€” no measured value yet, using literature estimates
Co-rotation drag reduction magnitude â€” theoretical but unverified for this geometry
unvalidated_assumptions: 3 m/s vessel velocity is achievable and maintained under hydrofoil drag load
Co-rotation of water body in 24ft cylinder is achievable and stable
Tack-flip mechanism can reverse hydrofoil angle without significant energy loss
competing_explanations: If hydrofoil L/D in this regime is low (less than 5), system may not reach 1.5W/W target
Co-rotation may require energy input to maintain, reducing net gain
disconfirming_observations: If complete system balance including all losses shows output/input less than 1.0, concept is not viable

position.last_activity_desc: v1.1 AoA Parametric Sweep milestone archived 2026-03-21 — NO_GO final verdict; AoA_optimal=2°; COP_max=1.210; design changes required for v1.2

open_questions: What design changes (depth, vessel count, geometry) are required to reach COP >= 1.5 — to be addressed in v1.2?
Tack-flip loss fraction (estimated +5% COP reduction) — highest prototype measurement priority
Can a deeper loop (e.g., 100 ft) or larger vessel count reach COP=1.5 at realistic compressor efficiency?

decisions.summary: Started milestone v1.1: AoA Parametric Sweep
decisions.rationale: New milestone cycle â€” v1.0 NO_GO verdict; reversed foil mounting is decisive design path

convention_lock.metric_signature: N/A â€” classical fluid mechanics project; no spacetime metric
convention_lock.fourier_convention: N/A â€” no Fourier transforms required in this project
convention_lock.natural_units: N/A â€” SI units throughout (m, kg, s, N, J, W, Pa)
convention_lock.gauge_choice: N/A â€” no gauge fields in this project
convention_lock.coordinate_system: z=0 at tank bottom; z=H=18.288m at water surface; z increases upward; P(z)=P_atm+rho_w*g*(H-z)
convention_lock.spin_basis: N/A â€” no quantum spin in this project
convention_lock.coupling_convention: N/A â€” no coupling constants in this project
convention_lock.index_positioning: N/A â€” no tensor index notation required
convention_lock.levi_civita_sign: N/A â€” no Levi-Civita tensor in this project
convention_lock.generator_normalization: N/A â€” no Lie algebra generators in this project
convention_lock.covariant_derivative_sign: N/A â€” no gauge-covariant derivatives in this project
convention_lock.gamma_matrix_convention: N/A â€” no Dirac matrices in this project

custom_conventions.unit_system: SI primary (m, kg, s, N, J, W, Pa); Imperial cross-checks in parentheses for design verification only
custom_conventions.rho_w: 998.2 kg/m^3 (fresh water 20 deg C; CRC Handbook)
custom_conventions.nu_w: 1.004e-6 m^2/s (kinematic viscosity, fresh water 20 deg C)
custom_conventions.V_depth: 0.07228 m^3 = 2.553 ft^3 = V_surface / P_r (Boyle's law fill volume)
custom_conventions.R_tank: 3.66 m (12 ft radius; 24 ft diameter cylinder)
custom_conventions.v_vessel: 3.0 m/s (preliminary; to be confirmed Phase 1 terminal velocity calculation)
custom_conventions.eta_target: 1.5 (target COP: 1.5 W shaft output per 1 W pumping input)
custom_conventions.W_iso: 20640 J = P_atm * V_surface * ln(P_r) (isothermal compression work per vessel)
custom_conventions.W_adia: 24040 J = 3.5 * P_atm * V_surface * (P_r^0.2857 - 1) (adiabatic compression work per vessel; gamma=1.4)
custom_conventions.gamma_air: 1.4 (heat capacity ratio for diatomic air; ideal gas)
custom_conventions.force_sign_convention: Positive = direction of useful work; F_b positive upward; F_D positive magnitude (enters as loss); F_L positive in shaft rotation direction
custom_conventions.energy_sign_convention: W_pump = input (denominator of COP); W_buoy, W_foil = outputs (numerator); W_drag, W_corot, W_friction = losses (subtracted from numerator)
custom_conventions.buoyancy_integral_rule: Always integrate F_b(z) = rho_w*g*V_surface*P_atm/P(z) over z=0 to H; NEVER use constant-volume F_b*H (74% overestimate)
custom_conventions.hydrofoil_force_convention: L = 0.5*rho_w*C_L_3D*A_foil*v_rel^2; D = 0.5*rho_w*(C_D_0+C_D_i)*A_foil*v_rel^2; v_rel = vessel velocity relative to water
custom_conventions.co_rotation_convention: v_rel_h = v_h*(1 - f_corot); vertical component v_rel_v = v_v unchanged; f_corot in [0,1]
custom_conventions.COP_formula: COP = (W_buoy + W_foil_up + W_foil_dn + W_jet - W_drag - W_D_foil - W_corot - W_friction) / W_pump
